trendOverride.reason: No trend override: quality 100/88, trend 75/100, sentiment neutral.

authenticity.notes: Authenticity check passed: article has enough specificity, business relevance, source alignment, and trend support.

notes: The article provides a practical, step-by-step guide to building AI workflows with memory for service businesses
Content is structured around a real business use case (Slack support triage) with clear implementation steps
Includes specific tools (Slack, Notion, OpenAI API, Zapier/Make) and measurable outcomes
Addresses common implementation challenges like API timeouts, low-confidence outputs, and duplicate ticket handling
Provides a detailed checklist for implementation and troubleshooting
recommendedFixes: Add more specific examples of prompts or API calls for the OpenAI integration to show how memory is actually implemented
Include a diagram or visual workflow to illustrate the end-to-end process described
Add a section on cost considerations for running these workflows (API costs, tooling costs)
Include specific error handling examples for common edge cases in workflow automation
Add a comparison table of different workflow automation tools (Zapier vs Make vs custom solutions)
